posts.author: Omkar_Ranjan
posts.text: Understanding Cords Cable’s Finance Cost – Is it really the issue? While analysing Cords Cable Industries, one aspect that initially caught my attention was the seemingly high finance cost relative to reported borrowings. Over FY17–FY26, reported debt has largely remained in the range of ₹70–115 crore , whereas annual interest and bank charges have consistently been around ₹22–26 crore . On the face of it, this appears unusually high. After going through the annual reports and notes to accounts, my understanding is that the apparent mismatch is largely explained by the company’s use of off- ba…
